message:
feat(random): RandomState.weibull legacy parity (br-franken_numpy-pl54)

Add weibull(a, size=None) to PyRandomState and the underlying
RandomState, matching numpy.random.RandomState.weibull under the
legacy MT19937 standard-exponential stream so draws are bit-for-bit
oracle-identical.

fnp-random:

- RandomState.weibull(a, size) -> Result<Vec<f64>, RandomError>.
  Rejects a < 0.0 or negative-zero (a == 0.0 && a.is_sign_negative())
  with RandomError::InvalidParameter — matches numpy which raises on
  a < 0 and on the -0.0 corner while returning all-zeros for +0.0.
  Short-circuits positive-zero to vec![0.0; size] so the stream
  pointer is NOT advanced, matching numpy's early-return behaviour.
  Otherwise draws `size` legacy standard-exponential samples and
  raises each one to the 1/a power, preserving the legacy stream
  ordering required for downstream reproducibility.

fnp-python:

- PyRandomState.weibull: pyo3 signature (a, size=None) returning the
  scalar-vs-array shape discipline shared with the rest of the
  wrappers via random_size_from_py / random_len_and_shape /
  build_random_f64_parts. Mirrors the rust-side validation error as
  PyValueError("a < 0").

Tests (fnp-random, via capture of numpy oracle values):

- shaped a=2.0, size=10 with seed 42: exact match against the 10
  captured floats, and post-draw random_interval stream state asserts
  the stream advanced by the expected amount.
- small_shape a=0.5, size=10 with seed 42: same approach, confirms
  the 1/a exponent path for sub-1 shape parameters.
- cached prefix: consume one standard_normal (which drains a cached
  normal) then call weibull to verify the legacy normal-cache does
  not contaminate the exponential stream; additional next_f64 draws
  afterward confirm expected stream positions.
- zero a: explicit vec![0.0; 3] and stream-unchanged assertion.
- NaN a: values must all be NaN, stream still advances.
- +INFINITY a: values must all be 1.0 (E^(1/inf) = 1), stream still
  advances.
- size=0: empty vec, stream unchanged.
- -0.0 a: InvalidParameter error, stream state preserved exactly.

Beads:

- franken_numpy-pl54 (in_progress): PORT random: RandomState legacy
  weibull parity. Parent-child dependency on franken_numpy-2h2s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Include oracle test covering: unweighted MaskedArray ignoring masked positions, weighted with same shape weights, returned=True yielding (avg, sum_of_weights), 2-D axis=0/1, fully-masked rows producing masked output, and plain ndarray matching np.average.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:51:29.201964438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:53:23.646613466Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:53:23.644721164Z","close_reason":"Completed: numpy.ma.average wrapper atomic landing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-pqam","title":"[MEDIUM] fnp-conformance fixtures/PROVENANCE.md missing (45 fixture files, 6 documented)","description":"per /testing-conformance-harnesses skill: 'fixture provenance is non-negotiable'. Every fixture must record how it was generated (tool + version + command + source-commit).

Current state:
- 45 JSON fixture files in crates/fnp-conformance/fixtures/*.json
- COVERAGE.md has a 'Fixture Provenance' table with 6 entries documented (ufunc_input_cases, signal_differential_cases, polynomial_differential_cases, dtype_promotion_cases, shape_stride_cases, runtime_policy_cases)
- 39 fixture files undocumented (datetime_*, fft_*, io_*, iter_*, linalg_*, masked_*, rng_*, string_*, ufunc_adversarial_cases, workflow_scenarios_*, etc.)

Gap impact: when fixtures regenerate in 6 months and results change, there is no record of what numpy version / oracle-capture script / git-commit generated them — cannot diagnose whether a result-change is a bug or a new numpy feature.

Required fix: create crates/fnp-conformance/fixtures/PROVENANCE.md with one row per fixture:
  - File name
  - Generator (manual / capture_numpy_oracle / dump_expected)
  - NumPy version used
  - Source git ref at generation time
  - Command invocation
  - Last regenerated date

Template from skill:
  | File                           | Generator       | NumPy | Command                          | Last regen |
  |--------------------------------|-----------------|-------|----------------------------------|------------|
  | ufunc_input_cases.json         | capture_numpy_oracle | 2.4.3 | cargo run --bin capture_numpy_oracle | 2026-04-15 |
  | ...                            | ...             | ...   | ...                              | ...        |

Priority rationale: MEDIUM — blocks reliable fixture regeneration workflow but does not affect current tests. Skill says this is 'non-negotiable' for claiming conformance.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:34:21.666569276Z","created_by":"cc-numpy","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:31:57.663180575Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:31:57.661262395Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","docs","review"]}
